channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fans, Athletes, and Public Figures Pay Final Respects to Rene Clert Baterbonia in Agusan del Sur
item.description: Hundreds of mourners gathered in Talacogon, Agusan del Sur on June 23, 2026, to pay their final respects to the late Ateneo basketball player Rene Clert Baterbonia during the last day of his wake. Sup...

item.title: Elizabeth Warren and Thom Tillis Clash During Senate Banking Committee Hearing on Credit Card Rate Caps
item.description: A Senate Banking Committee hearing on housing and consumer affordability became contentious when Senator Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts and Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina engaged in a heated...

item.title: Report Says Trump Used Former Jared Kushner Office to Store Personal Memorabilia and Gifts
item.description: A report based on the upcoming book 'Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump' by journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan claims that President Donald Trump has been using t...

item.title: MS NOW Segment Examines Concerns Over Trump's Reported Iran Oil and Sanctions Arrangement
item.description: The article focuses on comments made by MS NOW host Stephanie Ruhle regarding reports that the Trump administration would allow Iran to sell oil and receive payment in U.S. dollars as part of ongoing ...

item.title: Trump's $14 Million Reflecting Pool Renovation Plagued by Algae and Liner Issues Ahead of July 4
item.description: President Donald Trump has become increasingly focused on the troubled renovation of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., which he ordered painted blue as part of preparations for...

item.title: Court grants six-day physical remand of four suspects in Sargodha child murder investigation
item.description: A local court in Sargodha has handed over four suspects to police on a six-day physical remand in connection with the alleged attempted rape and murder of a seven-year-old girl. The decision was made ...

item.title: Talib Kweli Rejects Online Boxing Challenge as Social Media Feud Escalates
item.description: Rapper Talib Kweli became involved in a heated online dispute after a Facebook user, Jay Artem, publicly challenged him to a boxing match. Artem announced what he described as the first day of repeate...

item.title: Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan Dies at 100 After Shaping U.S. Economic Policy for Decades
item.description: Alan Greenspan, the influential economist who served five terms as chairman of the U.S. Federal Reserve, died at the age of 100 from complications related to Parkinson’s disease, according to his wife...

item.title: Rare 1956 LIFE Magazine Photographs Capture June Carter at the Height of Her Early Nashville Career
item.description: A collection of photographs taken in 1956 by LIFE magazine photographer Yale Joel offers a remarkable look at country music performer June Carter during a pivotal period in her career. The images were...

item.title: Former UN Representative Claims X Restricted His Account After Posts About Elon Musk and Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: A former United Nations representative and human rights diplomat, Mohamad Safa, publicly accused Elon Musk of restricting the visibility of his account on the social media platform X after he posted c...

item.title: President Pezeshkian Visits Pakistan for High-Level Talks on Bilateral Relations and Regional Cooperation
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian arrived in Pakistan on an official visit at the invitation of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and was received with full state honours in Islamabad. Upon his arrival ...

item.title: A Look at Stuart Sutcliffe’s Early Role in The Beatles Through Historic 1960s Photographs
item.description: The article highlights a collection of historical photographs featuring Stuart Sutcliffe alongside The Beatles during the group's formative years in the early 1960s. Sutcliffe, born in 1940, was a Bri...

item.title: Alexa Ilacad, Gelo Rivera, and Baby Dolls promote iWant and DITO’s ‘Libreng Manood DITO’ campaign on It’s Showtime
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios reported on the participation of Alexa Ilacad, Gelo Rivera, and the Baby Dolls group in the promotion of the ‘Libreng Manood DITO’ campaign, a joint initiative between iWant and DITO T...

item.title: Major Developments Across Africa: South Africa Protest Preparations, Kenya Ebola Facility Dispute, DRC Ebola Crisis, Food Security Concerns and HIV Funding Warnings
item.description: This AllAfrica roundup highlights several major developments across the continent on 23 June 2026. In South Africa, authorities have allocated significant resources and heightened security ahead of pl...

item.title: Opinion writer argues upcoming Senate questioning of Todd Blanche could increase scrutiny of the administration's handling of Epstein-related records
item.description: This article is an opinion piece by Tara Dublin published by Raw Story. The author argues that multiple controversies involving President Donald Trump have failed to divert public attention from ongoi...

item.title: Uganda High Court to Deliver July 7 Ruling in Dispute Over Suspension and Expulsion of Rotary Club Members
item.description: The High Court Civil Division in Kampala has scheduled July 7, 2026, to issue a ruling in a case involving five members of the Rotary Club of Kampala Ssese Islands who are contesting their suspension ...

item.title: A Retrospective on Jacqueline Sassard’s Career and Legacy in European Cinema
item.description: The article looks back at the life and career of French-born actress Jacqueline Sassard (1940–2021), a performer remembered for her elegance and understated presence in European cinema. Although she w...

item.title: Pastor James Ng’ang’a Defends Church Land Ownership and Awaits Court Decision
item.description: Pastor James Ng’ang’a of Neno Evangelism Centre has publicly maintained that he will not surrender in an ongoing dispute involving church land that he says was acquired legally. While addressing congr...

item.title: Former DOJ Attorney Alleges Policy Changes Allowed Special Treatment for Ghislaine Maxwell
item.description: A report by former Obama administration pardon attorney Liz Oyer alleges that the Trump administration's Department of Justice altered long-standing Bureau of Prisons (BOP) policies in a way that coul...

item.title: Analysis Projects Ohio Could Lose More Than 51,000 Jobs and Billions in Economic Output Due to Medicaid and SNAP Cuts
item.description: A new analysis from the Commonwealth Fund estimates that Ohio could lose approximately 51,200 jobs and more than $5 billion in economic activity by 2029 as a result of federal policy changes enacted u...
